reasoning:

My searches confirm this single event was very likely a bombing in May 2025 related to an anarchist anniversary (the "Day of Chaos"). The use of explosives by Mapuche groups in the south is rare, with most of their actions involving arson (which ACLED typically classifies as Riots or Strategic Developments).

For the first resolution date (Nov 2, 2025), the 30-day window is Oct 3 to Nov 2. As of the Oct 26 knowledge cutoff, there are no reported bombings in Chile. The remaining 7-day window carries a very small probability (~2%), and alongside a minor risk of a missed event, the probability is effectively at the 0.05 minimum boundary. 

For the second resolution date (Nov 25, 2025), the window is mostly after the knowledge cutoff, representing a standard 30-day period. The historical probability of an event in any given 30-day window is ~8.3%, so I estimate 9%.

For the final resolution date in January 2026, the 30-day window is entirely in the future. Drawing slightly closer to 0.5 to reflect the increased forecast horizon uncertainty, I estimate 11%. None of the upcoming periods heavily overlap with significant anarchist bombing anniversaries (e.g., in May or September), leaving the base rate as the strongest predictor.
